title: Israeli Airstrikes Kill at Least 32 in Gaza Including 7 Children
detail: Israeli airstrikes on April 1, 2026 killed at least 32 people including 7 children and injured more than 30 across Gaza. One strike directly hit a police station in the Sheikh Radwan neighborhood of Gaza City, killing 7 people including 4 female police officers. The IDF stated it targeted Hamas and Islamic Jihad commanders, a weapons depot, an arms manufacturing site, and two rocket launch positions. Palestinian civil defense confirmed the casualties. Al Jazeera and international media characterized the strikes as violations of the October 10, 2025 ceasefire. This was one of the deadliest single days of Israeli strikes in Gaza since the ceasefire took effect.

media.caption: Israeli airstrikes killed at least 32 people in Gaza on April 1, 2026

title: Only 40% of Mandated Aid Trucks Entered Gaza; UN Agency Shipments Suspended
detail: As of early April 2026, only 38,358 of 94,800 allocated trucks had entered Gaza since the October 10, 2025 ceasefire — just 40% of the agreed amount. Israel's COGAT suspended all UNDP shipments on March 25 after discovering engine oil (a prohibited dual-use item) in UNDP crates, and had separately suspended UNICEF shipments on March 18 after finding smuggled tobacco products. Meanwhile, OCHA documented rodent infestations spreading across displacement sites and urgent unmet needs for shelter, water, and sanitation. The Gaza Government Media Office had documented at least 2,073 Israeli ceasefire violations, 750 of which involved shootings at civilians.
